name: Ensure unencrypted LDAP port (389) is not exposed to the internet
body:
### Overview

This control identifies cases where an AWS security group allows inbound TCP/UDP port 389 from `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`. LDAP on port 389 is unencrypted by default and should not be reachable from the internet.

Use LDAPS on port 636 or private network access instead.
description: AWS security groups that expose unencrypted LDAP port 389 to the internet.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

### AWS

Remove internet-facing inbound access to unencrypted LDAP on TCP port 389. The check verifies AWS security group inbound rules whose port range includes 389 and source includes 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0.

1. Open **EC2 > Security Groups** and select the affected security group.
2. Review inbound rules where the port range includes 389.
3. Delete rules with source 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0.
4. If LDAP is required, restrict source CIDRs to trusted private networks only.
5. Prefer LDAPS on TCP 636 or a private directory access pattern instead of exposing LDAP 389.
6. Re-run sync and assessment.

Remove public IPv4 LDAP ingress:

~~~bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id {{asset.idFromProvider}} \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 389 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

Remove public IPv6 LDAP ingress:

~~~bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id {{asset.idFromProvider}} \
  --ip-permissions 'IpProtocol=tcp,FromPort=389,ToPort=389,Ipv6Ranges=[{CidrIpv6=::/0}]' \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

Validate remaining rules:

~~~bash
aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
  --group-ids {{asset.idFromProvider}} \
  --region {{asset.region}} \
  --query 'SecurityGroups[0].IpPermissions'
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/security-group-rules.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/revoke-security-group-ingress.html

The control is satisfied when no inbound rule on the security group exposes TCP 389 to the internet.
